rulings.Case Title: HUMBERTO RUIZ JR VS. ALFONSO RUIZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Hearing On Request For Writ Of Immediate Possession
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday February 23, 2012 line 2 - PLAINTIFF HUMBERTO RUIZ's Motion For Hearing On Request For Writ Of Immediate Possession. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party may re-notice motion but must give new notice of motion and hearing per CCP §§1005 et seq. and comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERIKA MCCARTNEY, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF, A CLASS OF VS. GAMESTOP CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 23, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT OCEANIC BANK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for plaintiff to plead facts specifying how she was compelled to publish the defamation (e.g. to prospective employers), to whom, where and when. Plaintiff concedes she cannot now state a cause of action for written defamation or libel.
